description_long: This is a tall, amphora-style vase with a rounded body, narrow neck, and two vertical handles extending from the shoulder to just below the rim. The surface is a pale, off-white color with visible cracks and patchwork repairs, indicating it has been reconstructed from several broken pieces. The rim is slightly flared, and the base is small and circular, providing a stable footing. The handles are thick and robust, seamlessly integrated into the body, and show some discoloration and wear. The overall finish is matte, with a slightly rough texture due to the visible seams and aged surface. There are no decorative motifs or painted patterns, giving the vase a simple, utilitarian appearance.
description: A tall, pale amphora-style vase with two sturdy handles, visible cracks, and a matte, aged surface. The body is rounded and reconstructed from several pieces, giving it a patchwork look.
description_view_0: This view highlights the left handle and the patchwork cracks on the upper left body, with the rim and neck clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view shows both handles symmetrically and the central seam lines running vertically and horizontally across the body.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the right handle and the extensive crack lines on the right side of the vase, with the neck and rim in clear profile.
description_view_3: This view presents both handles and the most extensive network of cracks and repairs, especially on the upper and central body.
